page: Amazon Web Services m7a.large is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs, providing a dedicated CPU allocation. It includes 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Single-threaded tasks like bzip3 decompression and text processing also demonstrate strong results. However, multi-core and memory-bound operations show limitations. Multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores fall into the average and weak categories respectively, reflecting the 2-vCPU constraint. Memory bandwidth benchmarks, particularly read-only operations, are weak to poor, with Passmark memory scores in the bottom 10%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m7a.large offers a modern DDR5 memory architecture and dedicated AMD EPYC cores at an entry-level resource scale, making it a viable option for workloads requiring strong single-thread performance without massive memory or parallel processing demands. It is suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, basic text processing, single-threaded utility scripts, and small-scale application hosting. It is less suitable for memory-intensive databases, heavy multi-threaded compilation, or large language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.large is a general-purpose instance featuring an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers strong single-core performance, as evidenced by high stress-ng and Geekbench single-core benchmark results. However, its multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth are relatively weak. This hardware profile makes the m7a.large qualitatively cost-effective for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic text processing, and small-scale application hosting, while it remains constrained for memory-intensive databases or parallelized computing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance, suitable for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.large features 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance for general-purpose tasks.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core AMD EPYC instance delivering strong single-threaded performance and DDR5 memory for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 2 dedicated cores.
Equipped with 8.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory.
Strong single-core and single-threaded benchmark performance.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included.
Delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.
Best suited for lightweight application hosting and text processing.
